message:
fix(abi): propagate errno on membrane Deny paths and add fallback allocation tracking

ABI shims previously returned null/error silently when the membrane
policy denied a call, leaving errno unset. Callers that check errno
(e.g., getpwnam, getgrnam, fopen) would see stale values and
misattribute failures.

Add set_abi_errno() helpers to grp, pwd, process, resolv, pthread,
stdio, time, and wchar ABI modules that set errno = EACCES on every
Deny path, giving callers a clear signal that the operation was
policy-blocked rather than a system error.

In malloc_abi, add a lock-free open-addressed hash table
(FALLBACK_ALLOC_TABLE_SLOTS = 4096 AtomicUsize slots) to track which
allocations went through native libc (bootstrap/reentrant paths) vs
the membrane arena. Uses multiplicative hashing, CAS-based insertion
with tombstone reuse, and CAS-based removal. This allows free/realloc
to route deallocations to the correct allocator, preventing
use-after-free and double-free when mixing allocation sources.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
